summary: Design and operate highly-available, high-throughput services and ETL pipelines for live sports streaming at global scale — from ML-driven event monitoring to developer self-service tooling.
highlights: Engineer highly-available, high-throughput backend services on AWS that hold up under live-event traffic spikes — stress-tested in production during Black Friday Football.
Design and operate ETL pipelines that ingest, transform, and serve large volumes of catalog and event data across the Prime Video Sports platform.
Built a scalable VLM-based recognition system for automated event monitoring, deployed catalog-wide.
Drove a cost-optimization effort that reclaimed over $350K in underutilized AWS resources in the first year.
Shipped a self-service image-slate portal and championed AI-assisted log diagnosis, cutting onboarding from two developer-days to none and enabling self-service root-causing for other teams.

summary: Built the internal developer platform — identity, observability, and CI/CD — and led the on-prem to AWS migration for engineering teams firm-wide.
highlights: Architected the firm's transition to Okta with in-house abstractions for other developers, reducing onboarding from several weeks to two days.
Onboarded Datadog and built migration tooling from legacy monitoring platforms, cutting mean-time-to-root-cause from 9 days to 2.
Created CI/CD pipelines that made full Okta and Datadog adoption a one-commit change for most applications in the firm.
Drove planning and execution of the on-premise to AWS migration, architecting bespoke solutions for internal teams as needed.
